title: Daily Market Digest: Geopolitical Tensions Weigh on Sentiment
subtitle: Market insights as of April 11, 2026

items.headline: U.S. Markets Close Mixed Amid Geopolitical Concerns and Inflation Data
items.summary: U.S. equities closed a choppy session on Friday, April 10, largely mixed as investors digested new inflation data and continued to monitor the fragile U.S.-Iran ceasefire talks. The S&P 500 and Dow Jones saw slight declines, while the Nasdaq managed a modest gain. Overall market sentiment remains cautious, influenced by persistent geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and their impact on global supply chains and energy prices, alongside a significant dip in consumer sentiment.

items.statContext: Down 0.1% for the day. For the week, the S&P 500 surged over 3%.
Up 0.4% for the day. The Nasdaq is set to climb more than 4% for the week.
Down 0.6% for the day. The Dow has advanced roughly 3% for the week.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Holds Rates Steady, Chair Transition Looms
items.summary: The Federal Reserve has maintained its federal funds rate at 3.50% to 3.75%. The next FOMC meeting is scheduled for April 28, potentially marking Jerome Powell's final meeting as Chair before Kevin Warsh assumes the role on May 15. The market is currently pricing in one to two rate cuts for 2026, potentially delayed until September or later.

items.headline: March CPI Jumps 0.9%, Driven by Energy Prices
items.summary: The Consumer Price Index (CPI) surged by 0.9% in March, primarily fueled by a 12.5% year-over-year increase in energy prices, with the gasoline index alone jumping 21.2% over the month. However, core CPI, excluding volatile food and energy components, rose by a more modest 0.2%, falling below consensus estimates.

items.headline: Consumer Sentiment Sinks Amid Iran Conflict Concerns
items.summary: Consumer sentiment in April plunged approximately 11%, extending a decline initiated by the Middle East conflict and now stands about 9% below year-ago levels. Consumers cited increased concerns over high prices and weakened asset values. Year-ahead inflation expectations surged from 3.8% in March to 4.8% in April, the largest one-month increase since April 2025.

items.headline: Middle East Conflict Causes Global Supply Chain Chaos and Economic Scars
items.summary: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East has led to chaos in global supply chains and a warning from the IMF head, Kristalina Georgieva, that the war will permanently scar the global economy. This has created diesel and jet fuel shortages and disrupted a fragile global energy system. The Bank of England governor also warned of a potential 2008-style financial crisis stemming from the private credit market due to the conflict.

items.headline: Nike Reports Flat Q3 Revenue, Net Income Down 35%
items.summary: Nike ($NKE) released its Q3 earnings last week, reporting flat revenues at $11.3 billion, a 3% decline on a currency-neutral basis. Net income fell by 35% year-over-year to $520 million, and gross margin declined by 130 basis points due to tariff-related product costs. The stock dropped over 8% following the report despite beating EPS estimates.

items.headline: PPL Releases FY 2026 Earnings Guidance
items.summary: PPL ($PPL) updated its fiscal year 2026 earnings guidance on Saturday morning, providing an EPS guidance of $1.90-$1.98, aligning with the consensus estimate of $1.95.
